title: Remove images from a registry using Podman
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
If we are pushing image with certain tag to container registry, then we should be able to delete images from remote by passing credentials using REST APIs

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: We a better description then this?  You want podman to be able to do something like `skopeo --delete`
A more complete usecase is definitely required here.
@rhatdan Is there any Rest API Support such that once Image is pushed to remote container registry like GCR, ACR. I can delete images from these registries ?

[Skopeo](https://github.com/containers/skopeo) supports deleting images from a registry (`skopeo delete docker://$image`).  With Skopeo being able to run in a container and the officially supported image on `quay.io/containers/skopeo`, you could run it in a Podman container via the REST API; assuming the REST API is important to your use case.

But are you asking the right question?  Do you want to delete the image from the local store after successfully pushing it to a registry?  If that is really what you are after, then yes there are APIs for this.

Sorry for this short description, Actually we are trying to use pod man in kubernetes environments to programmatically build images and pushing it to remote container registry. This remote container registry can be anything like ACR, GCR, docker private registry etc. I am not concerned about images stored locally, it will go away as new pod will replace old one, i want to delete images anytime which is stored in remote container registry programmatically using rest API
Currently Skopeo can do this, with `skopeo delete` iff the server is configured to allow users to delete images.  Not all registries come configured that way.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Feels awkward to not expose functionality we're adding, but I won't argue too loudly against it.

Why wouldn't I want the following?
`podman rmi --remote quay.io/rhatdan/badimage`
`podman image rm --remote quay.io/rhatdan/badimage`

I think they would be valuable.

Although --remote might cause confusion.  We would need to use a different flag.
podman image rm --registry quay.io/rhatdan/badimage?
I would mirror Git here - `podman push --delete`
I would be fine with that also.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@lsm5 would you be interested in doing this?

Just checked with the team and the consensus is that this feature should live inside skopeo along with a docs/manpage update in podman pointing users to skopeo. Correct me if I'm wrong. I can send a PR for it.
Yes lets just document that skopeo has this feature in the podman image rm man page.
Looks like the manpage hasnt been updated yet, should I do that? [This](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-rmi.1.md) is the correct one right?
Yes please fix it.
Friendly ping.  Who wants to open a PR for it?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@umohnani8 PTAL
